section-70.content: (1)Subject to this section and sections 71 to 75, nothing in or made under this Part applies to, or in relation to, a child or young person detained in pursuance of—/n(a)an order made by a court, or/n(b)an order of recall made by the Secretary of State./n(2)Subsection (1) does not apply to—/n(a)section 28;/n(b)section 31;/n(c)section 77;/n(d)section 80;/n(e)section 83;/n(f)any amendment made by this Part of a provision which applies to, or in relation to, a child or young person detained in pursuance of—/n(i)an order made by a court, or/n(ii)an order of recall made by the Secretary of State./n(3)Regulations may apply any provision of this Part, with or without modifications, to or in relation to a child or young person detained in pursuance of—/n(a)an order made by a court, or/n(b)an order of recall made by the Secretary of State./n(4)The Secretary of State must consult the Welsh Ministers before making regulations under subsection (3) which will apply any provision of this Part to, or in relation to, a child or young person who is detained in Wales./n(5)For the purposes of this Part—/n(6)For the purposes of this Part—/n(a)“beginning of the detention” has the same meaning as in Chapter 5A of Part 10 of EA 1996 (persons detained in youth accommodation), and/n(b)“the home authority” has the same meaning as in that Chapter, subject to regulations under subsection (7) (and regulations under section 562J(4) of EA 1996 made by the Secretary of State may also make provision in relation to the definition of “the home authority” for the purposes of this Part)./n(7)For the purposes of this Part, regulations may provide for paragraph (a) of the definition of “the home authority” in section 562J(1) of EA 1996 (the home authority of a looked after child) to apply with modifications in relation to such provisions of this Part as may be specified in the regulations.

section-71.title: Assessment of post-detention education, health and care needs of detained persons
section-71.content: (1)This section applies in relation to a detained person for whom—/n(a)the home authority is a local authority in England, and/n(b)no EHC plan is being kept by a local authority./n(2)A request to the home authority to secure a detained person's EHC needs assessment for the detained person may be made by—/n(a)the appropriate person, or/n(b)the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained./n(3)Where this subsection applies, the home authority must determine whether it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention./n(4)Subsection (3) applies where—/n(a)a request is made under subsection (2),/n(b)the detained person has been brought to the home authority's attention by any person as someone who has or may have special educational needs, or/n(c)the detained person has otherwise come to the home authority's attention as someone who has or may have special educational needs./n(5)In making a determination under subsection (3), the home authority must consult—/n(a)the appropriate person, and/n(b)the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained./n(6)Where the home authority determines that it will not be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention, it must notify the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained—/n(a)of the reasons for that determination, and/n(b)that accordingly it has decided not to secure a detained person's EHC needs assessment for the detained person./n(7)Subsection (8) applies where—/n(a)the detained person has not been assessed under this section or section 36 during the previous six months, and/n(b)the home authority determines that it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention./n(8)The home authority must notify the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained—/n(a)that it is considering securing a detained person's EHC needs assessment for the detained person, and/n(b)that the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained each have the right to—/n(i)express views to the authority (orally or in writing), and/n(ii)submit evidence to the authority./n(9)The home authority must secure a detained person's EHC needs assessment if, after having regard to any views expressed and evidence submitted under subsection (8), the authority is of the opinion that—/n(a)the detained person has or may have special educational needs, and/n(b)it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention./n(10)After a detained person's EHC needs assessment has been carried out, the local authority must notify the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained of—/n(a)the outcome of the assessment,/n(b)whether it proposes to secure that an EHC plan is prepared for the detained person, and/n(c)the reasons for that decision./n(11)Regulations may make provision about detained persons' EHC needs assessments, in particular—/n(a)about requests under subsection (2);/n(b)imposing time limits in relation to consultation under subsection (5);/n(c)about giving notice;/n(d)about expressing views and submitting evidence under subsection (8);/n(e)about how detained persons' EHC needs assessments are to be conducted;/n(f)about advice to be obtained in connection with a detained person's EHC needs assessment;/n(g)about combining a detained person's EHC needs assessment with other assessments;/n(h)about the use for the purposes of a detained person's EHC needs assessment of information obtained as a result of other assessments;/n(i)about the use of information obtained as a result of a detained person's EHC needs assessment, including the use of that information for the purposes of other assessments;/n(j)about the provision of information, advice and support in connection with a detained person's EHC needs assessment.

section-72.title: Securing EHC plans for certain detained persons
section-72.content: (1)Where, in the light of a detained person's EHC needs assessment it is necessary for special education provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention, the home authority must secure that an EHC plan is prepared for him or her./n(2)Sections 37(2) to (5) and 38 to 40 apply in relation to an EHC plan secured under subsection (1) as they apply to an EHC plan secured under section 37(1), with the following modifications—/n(a)references to “the child or young person” are to be read as references to the detained person,/n(b)references to the local authority are to be read as references to the home authority, and/n(c)references to the child's parent or the young person are to be read as references to the appropriate person./n(3)Section 33(2) to (7) apply where a home authority is securing the preparation of an EHC plan under this section as they apply where a local authority is securing a plan under section 37, with the following modifications—/n(a)references to “the child or young person” are to be read as references to the detained person,/n(b)references to the local authority are to be read as references to the home authority,/n(c)references to the child's parent or the young person are to be read as references to the appropriate person, and/n(d)the reference in subsection (2) to section 39(5) and 40(2) is to be read as a reference to those provisions as applied by subsection (2) of this section.

section-73.title: EHC plans for certain detained persons: appeals and mediation
section-73.content: (1)An appropriate person in relation to a detained person may appeal to the First-tier Tribunal against the matters set out in subsection (2), subject to section 55 (as applied by this section)./n(2)The matters are—/n(a)a decision of the home authority not to secure a detained person's EHC needs assessment for the detained person;/n(b)a decision of the home authority, following a detained person's EHC needs assessment, that it is not necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention;/n(c)where an EHC plan is secured for the detained person—/n(i)the school or other institution named in the plan, or the type of school or other institution named in the plan;/n(ii)if no school or other institution is named in the plan, that fact./n(3)The appropriate person may appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under subsection (2)(c) only when an EHC plan is first finalised for the detained person in accordance with section 72./n(4)Regulations may make provision about appeals to the First-tier Tribunal in respect of detained persons' EHC needs assessments and EHC plans secured under section 72, in particular about—/n(a)making and determining appeals;/n(b)the powers of the First-tier Tribunal on determining an appeal;/n(c)unopposed appeals./n(5)A person commits an offence if without reasonable excuse that person fails to comply with any requirement—/n(a)in respect of the discovery or inspection of documents, or/n(b)to attend to give evidence and produce documents,/nwhere that requirement is imposed by Tribunal Procedure Rules in relation to an appeal under this section./n(6)A person guilty of an offence under subsection (5) is liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale./n(7)Section 55(2) to (5) apply where an appropriate person intends to appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under this section as they apply where a child's parent or young person intends to appeal under section 51, with the following modifications—/n(a)references to the child's parent or young person are to be read as references to the appropriate person, and/n(b)references to mediation under section 53 or 54 are to be read as references to mediation with the home authority./n(8)Where, by virtue of subsection (7), the appropriate person has informed the mediation adviser that he or she wishes to pursue mediation with the home authority—/n(a)the adviser must notify the authority, and/n(b)the authority must—/n(i)arrange for mediation between it and the appropriate person,/n(ii)ensure that the mediation is conducted by an independent person, and/n(iii)participate in the mediation./nFor this purpose a person is not independent if he or she is employed by a local authority in England./n(9)Regulations under section 56 may make provision for the purposes of subsections (7) and (8) of this section, and accordingly section 56 has effect for those purposes with the following modifications—/n(a)the references in subsection (1) to commissioning bodies are to be ignored;/n(b)the reference in subsection (1)(e) to a child's parent is to be read as a reference to the parent of a detained person who is a child;/n(c)the reference in subsection (1)(f) to the child's parent or young person is to be read as a reference to the appropriate person;/n(d)in subsection (3), paragraphs (b) and (c) are to be ignored;/n(e)subsection (4) is to be ignored.

section-74.title: Duty to keep EHC plans for detained persons
section-74.content: (1)This section applies in relation to a detained person—/n(a)for whom a local authority in England was maintaining an EHC plan immediately before the beginning of his or her detention, or/n(b)for whom the home authority has secured the preparation of an EHC plan under section 72./n(2)The home authority must keep the EHC plan while the person is detained in relevant youth accommodation./n(3)Regulations may make provision about the keeping of EHC plans under subsection (2), and the disclosure of such plans./n(4)The home authority must arrange appropriate special educational provision for the detained person while he or she is detained in relevant youth accommodation./n(5)If the EHC plan specifies health care provision, the detained person's health services commissioner must arrange appropriate health care provision for the detained person while he or she is detained in relevant youth accommodation./n(6)For the purposes of subsection (4), appropriate special educational provision is—/n(a)the special educational provision specified in the EHC plan, or/n(b)if it appears to the home authority that it is not practicable for that special educational provision to be provided, educational provision corresponding as closely as possible to that special educational provision, or/n(c)if it appears to the home authority that the special educational provision specified in the plan is no longer appropriate for the person, such special educational provision as reasonably appears to the home authority to be appropriate./n(7)For the purposes of subsection (5), appropriate health care provision is—/n(a)the health care provision specified in the EHC plan, or/n(b)if it appears to the detained person's health services commissioner that it is not practicable for that health care provision to be provided, health care provision corresponding as closely as possible to that health care provision, or/n(c)if it appears to the detained person's health services commissioner that the health care provision specified in the plan is no longer appropriate for the person, such health care provision as reasonably appears to the detained person's health services commissioner to be appropriate./n(8)In this section, “detained person's health services commissioner”, in relation to a detained person, means the body that is under a duty under the National Health Service Act 2006 to arrange for the provision of services or facilities in respect of the detained person during his or her detention.

section-75.title: Supply of goods and services: detained persons
section-75.content: (1)A local authority in England may supply goods and services to any authority or other person making special educational provision for a detained person, but only for the purpose set out in subsection (2)./n(2)The purpose is that of assisting the local authority in the performance of a duty under section 74./n(3)The goods and services may be supplied on the terms and conditions that the authority thinks fit, including terms as to payment.

section-76.title: Provision and publication of special needs information
section-76.content: (1)The Secretary of State must exercise the powers listed in subsection (2) with a view to securing, in particular, the provision of special needs information which the Secretary of State thinks would be likely to assist the Secretary of State or others in improving the well-being of—/n(a)children in England with special educational needs, and/n(b)young people aged under 19 in England with special educational needs./n(2)The powers are those of the Secretary of State under the following provisions of EA 1996 (so far as relating to England)—/n(a)section 29 (information from local authorities for purposes of Secretary of State's functions);/n(b)section 408 (information in relation to maintained schools);/n(c)section 537 (information about schools);/n(d)section 537A (information about individual pupils);/n(e)section 537B (information about children receiving funded education outside school);/n(f)section 538 (information from governing bodies for purposes of Secretary of State's education functions)./n(3)In each calendar year, the Secretary of State must publish, or arrange to be published, special needs information which has been obtained under EA 1996, where the Secretary of State thinks the publication of the information would be likely to assist the Secretary of State or others in improving the well-being of —/n(a)children in England with special educational needs, and/n(b)young people aged under 19 in England with special educational needs./n(4)Information published under subsection (3) must be published in the form and manner that the Secretary of State thinks fit, except that the names of the children and young people to whom the information relates must not be included./n(5)The Secretary of State may make a charge, or arrange for a charge to be made, for documents supplied by virtue of this section./n(6)A charge under subsection (5) must not exceed the cost of supply./n(7)“Special needs information” means—/n(a)information about children, and young people, in England with special educational needs, and/n(b)information about special educational provision made for those children and young people./n(8)References in this section to the well-being of children and young people with special educational needs are to their well-being so far as relating to—/n(a)physical and mental health and emotional well-being;/n(b)protection from abuse and neglect;/n(c)control by them over their day-to-day lives;/n(d)participation in education, training or recreation;/n(e)social and economic well-being;/n(f)domestic, family and personal relationships;/n(g)the contribution made by them to society.

section-77.title: Code of practice
section-77.content: (1)The Secretary of State must issue a code of practice giving guidance about the exercise of their functions under this Part to—/n(a)local authorities in England;/n(b)the governing bodies of schools;/n(c)the governing bodies of institutions within the further education sector;/n(d)the proprietors of Academies;/n(e)the management committees of pupil referral units;/n(f)the proprietors of institutions approved by the Secretary of State under section 41 (independent special schools and special post-16 institutions: approval);/n(g)providers of relevant early years education;/n(h)youth offending teams;/n(i)persons in charge of relevant youth accommodation;/n(j)NHS England;/n(k)integrated care boards;/n(l)NHS trusts;/n(m)NHS foundation trusts;/n(n)Local Health Boards./n(2)The Secretary of State may revise the code from time to time./n(3)The Secretary of State must publish the current version of the code./n(4)The persons listed in subsection (1) must have regard to the code in exercising their functions under this Part./n(5)Those who exercise functions for the purpose of the exercise by those persons of functions under this Part must also have regard to the code./n(6)The First-tier Tribunal must have regard to any provision of the code that appears to it to be relevant to a question arising on an appeal under this Part.

section-78.title: Making and approval of code
section-78.content: (1)Where the Secretary of State proposes to issue or revise a code under section 77, the Secretary of State must prepare a draft of the code (or revised code)./n(2)The Secretary of State must consult such persons as the Secretary of State thinks fit about the draft and must consider any representations made by them./n(3)If the Secretary of State decides to proceed with the draft (in its original form or with modifications), the Secretary of State must lay a copy of the draft before each House of Parliament./n(4)The Secretary of State may not take any further steps in relation to—/n(a)a proposed code unless the draft is approved by a resolution of each House, or/n(b)a proposed revised code if, within the 40-day period, either House resolves not to approve the draft./n(5)Subsection (6) applies if—/n(a)both Houses resolve to approve the draft, as mentioned in subsection (4)(a), or/n(b)neither House resolves not to approve the draft, as mentioned in subsection (4)(b)./n(6)The Secretary of State must issue the code or revised code in the form of the draft, and it comes into force on such date as the Secretary of State may by order appoint./n(7)Subsection (4) does not prevent a new draft of a proposed code (or proposed revised code) from being laid before Parliament./n(8)In this section “40-day period”, in relation to the draft of a proposed revised code, means—/n(a)if the draft is laid before one House on a later day than the day on which it is laid before the other, the period of 40 days beginning with the later of the two days, and/n(b)in any other case, the period of 40 days beginning with the day on which the draft is laid before each House./n(9)For the purposes of subsection (8), no account is to be taken of any period during which Parliament is dissolved or prorogued or during which both Houses are adjourned for more than four days.

section-79.title: Review of resolution of disagreements
section-79.content: (1)The Secretary of State and the Lord Chancellor must carry out a review of how effectively disagreements about the exercise of functions under this Part are being resolved./n(2)The Secretary of State and the Lord Chancellor must prepare a report on the outcome of the review./n(3)The Secretary of State and the Lord Chancellor must lay the report before Parliament before the end of the period of three years beginning with the earliest date on which any provision of this Part comes into force.

section-80.title: Parents and young people lacking capacity
section-80.content: (1)Regulations may apply any statutory provision with modifications, for the purpose of giving effect to this Part in a case where the parent of a child, or a young person, lacks capacity at the relevant time./n(2)Regulations under subsection (1) may in particular include provision for—/n(a)references to a child's parent to be read as references to, or as including references to, a representative of the parent;/n(b)references to a young person to be read as references to, or as including references to, a representative of the young person, the young person's parent, or a representative of the young person's parent;/n(c)modifications to have effect in spite of section 27(1)(g) of the Mental Capacity Act 2005 (Act does not permit decisions on discharging parental responsibilities in matters not relating to a child's property to be made on a person's behalf)./n(3)“Statutory provision” means a provision made by or under this or any other Act, whenever passed or made./n(4)“The relevant time” means the time at which, under the statutory provision in question, something is required or permitted to be done by or in relation to the parent or young person./n(5)The reference in subsection (1) to lacking capacity is to lacking capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005./n(6)“Representative”, in relation to a parent or young person, means—/n(a)a deputy appointed by the Court of Protection under section 16(2)(b) of the Mental Capacity Act 2005 to make decisions on the parent's or young person's behalf in relation to matters within this Part;/n(b)the donee of a lasting power of attorney (within the meaning of section 9 of that Act) appointed by the parent or young person to make decisions on his or her behalf in relation to matters within this Part;/n(c)an attorney in whom an enduring power of attorney (within the meaning of Schedule 4 to that Act) created by the parent or young person is vested, where the power of attorney is registered in accordance with paragraphs 4 and 13 of that Schedule or an application for registration of the power of attorney has been made.

section-81.title: Disapplication of Chapter 1 of Part 4 of EA 1996 in relation to children in England
section-81.content: Chapter 1 of Part 4 of EA 1996 (children with special educational needs) ceases to apply in relation to children in the area of a local authority in England.

section-82.title: Consequential amendments
section-82.content: Schedule 3 (amendments consequential on this Part) has effect.

section-83.title: Interpretation of Part 3
section-83.content: (1)In this Part—/n(2)In this Part—/n(3)A child or young person has a disability for the purposes of this Part if he or she has a disability for the purposes of the Equality Act 2010./n(4)A reference in this Part to “education”—/n(a)includes a reference to full-time and part-time education, but/n(b)does not include a reference to higher education,/nand “educational” and “educate” (and other related terms) are to be read accordingly./n(5)A reference in this Part to—/n(a)a community, foundation or voluntary school, or/n(b)a community or foundation special school,/nis to such a school within the meaning of SSFA 1998./n(6)A reference in this Part to a child or young person who is “in the area” of a local authority in England does not include a child or young person who is wholly or mainly resident in the area of a local authority in Walesor who would be wholly or mainly resident in the area of a local authority in Wales were it not for provision secured for the child or young person under Part 2 of the Additional Learning Needs and Education Tribunal (Wales) Act 2018./n(7)EA 1996 and the preceding provisions of this Part (except so far as they amend other Acts) are to be read as if those provisions were contained in EA 1996.
